_comment: Zero-dependency absence floor: one committed checkpoint per agent — the highest confirmed on-chain anchor for that agent. Each is the immutable anchorBatch tx's own calldata (agentId, merkleRoot, lastSeq) cross-checked against the agent_audit_anchors record, with tx.to = YoloAuditAnchor 0xDf5e1c1e82880C0E9dce3758A58e62189Ca365FD and receipt status = success; block is the tx's mined block. Anyone can re-derive these by decoding each tx on Basescan (decodeFunctionData anchorBatch). Use: when a REACHABLE RPC reports no matching anchor for an entry whose agentId has a checkpoint here and whose seq is at/below that checkpoint's lastSeq, the absence is corroborated (the contract demonstrably anchored that agent past that point) — a hard anchor_absent, not RPC lag. Agents with no checkpoint fall back to the honest single-RPC label. Mirror of LAST_KNOWN_ANCHORS in lib/verify-client.ts, tied by test/verifier-reference-seed-sync.test.ts. NOTE: agent acce0541-0336-4a5c-a3ed-55dbfd59f1dd has anchor records but none with an on-chain tx_hash (its anchor row is the known corrupt merkle_root='ch'), so it is intentionally NOT checkpointed.
